behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names all specific file paths (framework-helpers.ts, workers-helpers.ts, mocks.ts, to-exist.ts, frameworks.test.ts, workers.test.ts), all function names (runC3ForFrameworkTest, verifyDeployment, etc.), the type ExpectStatic, the eslint-disable comment pattern, and the throw new Error() replacement. All test assertions reference items discoverable from the instruction.

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly prescribes the fix: add 'expect: ExpectStatic' as a typed parameter to specific functions, use type-only import for ExpectStatic, replace expect.fail() with throw new Error(), and pass expect as argument at call sites. This goes beyond describing what's broken — it specifies exactly what code changes to make.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests use regex like r'\bexpect\s*:' to check parameter names, requiring the parameter to be named exactly 'expect'. An alternative correct fix could use a different parameter name (e.g., 'expectFn') or use a different injection pattern (e.g., context object), but would fail the tests. Similarly, test_mocks_throw_error requires exactly 'throw new Error(' pattern — alternatives like 'throw new TypeError(' would not count.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The fail-to-pass tests (test_eslint_disable_comments_resolved, test_helpers_accept_expect_param, test_call_sites_pass_expect) are purely text-based: they read file contents and check regex patterns. They do not execute the code. Only test_mocks_throw_error uses node to parse text. The pass-to-pass tests (typecheck, unit tests, build) do execute code, but the core f2p tests are textual assertions.

test_not_tautological.reason: A stub/no-op implementation would fail because: (1) the base files still contain eslint-disable comments that test_eslint_disable_comments_resolved checks for absence of, (2) functions don't have expect parameters that test_helpers_accept_expect_param checks for, (3) mocks.ts still uses expect.fail() that test_mocks_throw_error rejects. The pass-to-pass typecheck test also ensures the code compiles.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution exists at /workspace/workers-sdk as a git repo, and the agent could potentially read the solution/solve.sh file if it exists in the container. The Dockerfile doesn't remove solution files. Also, the agent could grep test files to discover exact regex patterns expected.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behaviors described in the instruction are tested: eslint-disable removal (test_eslint_disable_comments_resolved), to-exist.ts justification, expect parameter injection (test_helpers_accept_expect_param), mocks.ts throw new Error (test_mocks_throw_error), call site updates (test_call_sites_pass_expect), and typecheck/build/unit tests pass.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with section comments (pass-to-pass, fail-to-pass), descriptive function names (test_eslint_disable_comments_resolved, test_helpers_accept_expect_param), and docstrings explaining what each test verifies.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task is a code refactoring task, not one that produces structured data output.

no_typos.reason: All file paths, function names, and variable names in the instruction match those referenced in the tests and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names exact files AND exact function signatures (e.g., 'accept expect as a typed parameter (e.g., expect: ExpectStatic)'), specific function lists per file, and exact replacement patterns (expect.fail() -> throw new Error()). This is essentially a complete implementation specification, not just a bug description.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a code-only refactoring task, not a config-edit task.
